name: (Natural) Rock Turn
preceding_figure_names: Natural Promenade Turn, Progressive Side Step, Walk, Progressive Link, Back Corte, LF Rock, Open Reverse Turn, Lady Outside (when preceding figure ended DC), Open Reverse Turn, Lady In Line (when preceding figure ended DC), Progressive Side Step Reverse Turn (when preceding figure ended DC), Reverse Outside Swivel (when preceding figure ended DC), Basic Reverse Turn (when preceding figure ended DC), Fallaway Reverse and Slip Pivot (when preceding figure ended DC; or normal), Four Step, Outside Swivel, Four Step Change, Brush Tap, Fallaway Four Step, Oversway, Five Step, Contra Check
following_figure_names: Walk (LF), Progressive Side Step, Progressive Link, Back Corte, LF Rock, Open Reverse Turn, Lady Outside (when preceding figure ended DC), Open Reverse Turn, Lady In Line (when preceding figure ended DC), Progressive Side Step Reverse Turn (when preceding figure ended DC), Reverse Outside Swivel (when preceding figure ended DC), Basic Reverse Turn (when preceding figure ended DC), Fallaway Reverse and Slip Pivot (when preceding figure ended DC; or normal), Four Step, Outside Swivel, Four Step Change, Brush Tap, Fallaway Four Step, Oversway, Five Step, Contra Check
notes: Rock Turn is a Newcomer Level figure. Detailed description includes timing, footwork, alignment, movement; steps diagram; list of preceding and following figures; videos providing instruction and demonstration of the figure, techniques and practice routines that include it.

comments: This step is straight.
Direction is confusing. Man is backing DC. (Coach is doing H.)

steps.action: back, small step, L side leading
comments: Man is backing DC, moving DC direction.
moving toward center, position is BC
Need to keep wide enough tracks so Man has enough room to step.
To be dramatic: Man lead Lady to go back strong with extra shaping from head/body.
Straight back.
During the rock QQS: no funny shoulder movement, keep shoulder flat.
Take center with the foot when rocking.
Rotate, but be very aware to stay left of Man, do not get into Man's space.

steps.action: back and slightly leftwards, L side leading
comments: Rocking action, legs positions are held.

steps.action: fwd, small step, R side leading
comments: There is more body turn to R to wind up.
Corte: keep center to Man, leave head behind, bigger step, rotate shoulder, center is very forward, extend and shape.
Stay very left throughout. Lady's left side is very forward to Man. (despite the "R side leading")
Head flick: "&S": timing is critical - sync up with Man, otherwise it looks awkward and artificial.
Head to R option: Lady opens up more, shape to R, head to R, but give R side to Man.
Weight should be on RF, not in middle. Man is stretching to his left side.
Do not initiate with body forward.
Use the R leg to drive the step.
Do not get into Man's space.
Step forward through Man.
After stepping onto LF, use left side as axis.
Keep body contact, stay with Man.
Do not turn shoulder too quickly to L.
